narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD broad weakness because DXY down 0.42%, USD ranked zero in CSM, and H4 sell signals on USDJPY and USDCHF are all pointing in the same direction with strong cross-pair confirmation.
2. CHF and commodity currency strength because CHF CSM at 100, NZD at 91, AUD at 84 are leading the G10 rankings and copper up 2.76% is directly endorsing the antipodean bid.
3. Risk-on equity and commodity surge because S&P500 up 0.84%, copper up 2.76%, and oil up 0.64% are collectively driving JPY crosses higher through the CADJPY-USDJPY correlation cluster at 0.93.

PRICE CONFIRMS

AUDUSD and NZDUSD are leading the USD weakness narrative with correlations to EURUSD above 0.87 and 0.88 respectively, confirming that the move is broad-based dollar selling rather than idiosyncratic pair flows. USDJPY carries a strong sell signal of minus 5 on H4 which is consistent with the JPY cross complex where EURJPY and GBPJPY are moving together at 0.97 correlation, but the JPY bid in USDJPY is diverging from AUDJPY and NZDJPY H4 buy signals, meaning the yen cross move is equity-driven carry rather than pure yen strength. USDCHF H4 sell at minus 4 confirms CHF dominance as the anti-dollar trade with the highest CSM score.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed with conflict on gold. S&P500 at 7399 up 0.84% and VIX at 17.19 rising only 0.64% point to orderly risk appetite, but gold at 4731 up 0.66% and silver up 1.46% introduce a hedging undercurrent that prevents a clean risk-on classification, though equity and copper strength outweigh the precious metals signal.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on: gold rising alongside equities and copper suggests residual macro fear is present beneath the surface risk appetite, which conflicts with a pure risk-on narrative.
2. CADJPY H4 sell signal versus USDJPY H4 sell signal: CAD is the weakest commodity currency at CSM 14 yet CADJPY correlates at 0.93 with USDJPY, making the CADJPY sell signal internally inconsistent with the broader JPY cross bid driven by equity strength.

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: AUDUSD up - CHF and AUD lead CSM, USD at zero, copper surge endorses AUD directly - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDUSD up - NZD CSM at 91, high correlation to AUD and GBP dollar weakness cluster confirmed - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: CADJPY - conflicting CSM signals and weakest commodity currency creates directional noise
Watch
key_observation: Headline set shows broad risk-on sentiment with equity strength, commodity recovery across agriculture complex, and resilient labor market data. However, limited direct FX commentary. Trump-Fed commentary suggests policy uncertainty. Overall environment favors risk currencies over USD despite labor market strength.
watch: Trump Fed policy impact. Commodity momentum sustainability. Equity rally durability. Labor market data consistency in upcoming week.
